title: Building from source fails with macOS
body:
I cloned the repo set the env variables correctly.

    $ make install

This fails with error:

    make: *** No rule to make target `skopeo', needed by `install-binary'.  Stop.

comments.body:
I just did the build from master, no issues:

```bash
brew install gpgme
git clone https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo $GOPATH/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo
cd $GOPATH/src/github.com/projectatomic/skopeo && make binary-local
go get github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man && go install github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man
make docs
sudo make install
```

works fine on OSX

or even with 0.1.23 and ostree stub:
```
git checkout v0.1.23
make binary-local BUILDTAGS=containers_image_ostree_stub
```

Got it, I was missing the binary-local target.

Would you consider creating a homebrew formula for this? `make install` sounds like it'll do things on my system I can't remove easily, i.e. via a package manager.

These resources can help:

- tool: https://jkawamoto.github.io/fgo/
- example formula: https://github.com/Homebrew/legacy-homebrew/blob/3c6951ca3072c76af19d096e1332c31de07426c6/Library/Formula/termshare.rb
- more links: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/27097005/how-to-package-go-project-for-homebrew
Sadly, (Really happily) I only have Linux systems to work on.  :^)
I might have a go at it, but priority is very low for me as the Makefiles work fine

> homebrew package Homebrew/homebrew-core#29982

Great, thanks! (FWIW there has already been a 0.1.30 tagged, apparently we have mismanaged the GitHub options for that. Marked as a release now, at least.)
